name: Somerville Bank
description_short: Ohio-based community bank established in 1910, operating 7 branches across Southwestern Ohio with a focus on local service, consumer and business lending, and merchant services.
description_long:
Somerville Bank was established in 1910 as Somerville National Bank, beginning as a small institution serving Southwestern Ohio. Until 1989, it held the distinction of being the smallest nationally chartered bank in the United States. The bank expanded from its original single location to seven full-service branches across Ohio by the present day. On January 1, 2018, the institution transitioned from a national charter to an Ohio charter and adopted its current name of Somerville Bank. The bank celebrated its 100th anniversary in April 2010 and continues to operate under the same ownership and leadership structure.

Somerville Bank offers a comprehensive suite of financial products for both consumer and business customers. On the consumer side, they provide checking and savings accounts, credit cards (recently added to their product line), residential lending options including conventional mortgages, FHA/VA loans, construction loans, investment property loans, HELOCs, auto loans, and specialized programs like USDA Rural Development loans. For business customers, the bank offers business accounts, business credit cards, commercial real estate loans, SBA loans, agricultural land loans, construction loans, and FSA loans. Additional services include online banking, mobile banking, Zelle integration, identity theft services, check ordering, and merchant services through a partnership with Clover point-of-sale systems.

Somerville Bank distinguishes itself through exceptional employee stability and local market expertise. All senior management have been with the bank for over 20 years, with most having 30+ years of banking experience primarily in Preble County, Ohio. Many employees maintain 10+ year tenure with the institution, creating deep community knowledge and continuity. The bank's stated mission emphasizes being a low-cost financial services provider while maintaining strong capital reserves and supporting community services. Customer testimonials consistently highlight the personal service quality, friendly staff, small-town relationship banking, and community commitment.

Somerville Bank is genuinely a community-focused regional institution with strong operational stability and local expertise. The primary limitation is geographic scope—with only 7 branches in Ohio, they serve a limited regional market and cannot offer the convenience or digital capabilities of larger national banks. Additionally, as a smaller regional bank, they may have fewer competitive rates on certain products and less extensive product customization compared to larger competitors. However, for customers in their service area seeking relationship banking, personalized service, and community-minded financial institutions, Somerville Bank represents a legitimate alternative to national chains. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Merchant services and Clover point-of-sale systems
pros: 116 years of continuous operation with strong employee tenure—all senior management have 20+ years with the bank and 30+ years banking experience
Recently added credit card products for both consumer and business customers, expanding their lending offerings
Comprehensive residential lending programs including conventional mortgages, construction loans, FHA/VA loans, USDA Rural Development, investment property, and HELOCs
Full-service business lending including SBA loans, agricultural land loans, commercial real estate, construction, and FSA loans
Integrated merchant services through Clover partnership offering point-of-sale systems, inventory management, payroll, and employee scheduling for business customers
Multiple service delivery channels including online banking, mobile banking, Zelle, and 7 full-service branches with ATMs
Consistent customer testimonials praising staff knowledge, personal service, community commitment, and relationship banking approach
cons: Limited geographic footprint—only 7 branches in Ohio, restricting accessibility and convenience for customers outside Southwestern Ohio
Smaller institution means potentially less competitive rates and pricing compared to national banks with greater economies of scale
Transitioned to Ohio state charter in 2018, potentially limiting some regulatory or product advantages of national banking
Limited information on website regarding specific rates, fees, or competitive product details compared to larger competitors
No indication of investment products, retirement accounts, or wealth management services beyond basic lending and deposit accounts
best_for: Small business owners and farmers in Southwestern Ohio seeking relationship banking and specialized agricultural/FSA lending
Residential borrowers in the bank's service area wanting personalized mortgage lending with local decision-making
Restaurants and retailers needing integrated point-of-sale and business management solutions through Clover partnership
Customers prioritizing community-focused banking relationships and local reinvestment over national bank convenience
diagnosis: Somerville Bank is best suited for small business owners, farmers, and residential customers within its Southwestern Ohio footprint who value personalized service, local decision-making, and community reinvestment over the convenience of national bank networks. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—this bank only serves customers in Ohio, making it inaccessible to those outside its 7-branch service area, and it lacks the extensive digital capabilities and competitive pricing power of larger national institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Somerville Bank is an Ohio community bank established in 1910 with 7 branches offering consumer and business lending.
